items.title: Gemini sued over post-IPO strategy shift, declining stock price
items.summary: Crypto exchange Gemini is facing a class-action lawsuit over what a complaint alleges is an “abrupt corporate pivot to a prediction-market-centric business model” after its IPO.

items.title: Bitcoin jumps to $70,800 as oil retreats; ether and XRP lag
items.summary: Oil prices slipped as major economies announced joint efforts to stabilize energy markets.

items.title: Appeals court denies Kalshi’s bid to stave off ban in Nevada
items.summary: US gaming lawyer Daniel Wallach says a Nevada state court-issued restraining order against Kalshi appears imminent, preventing it from offering sports-related contracts.

items.title: Morgan Stanley sets MSBT ticker and $1 million seed capital for bitcoin ETF
items.summary: Morgan Stanley has filed to launch a spot Bitcoin ETF with the ticker MSBT and a $1 million seed at debut.

items.title: Kentucky Senate Urged to Strip Hardware Wallet Provision From Crypto Bill
items.summary: A crypto kiosk bill in Kentucky includes language that could effectively outlaw self-custody, drawing industry backlash.

items.title: Major gold trade group releases framework for tokenized gold
items.summary: The World Gold Council says it will develop a platform to connect physical gold to the systems used to issue and manage tokenized gold products.

items.title: Nvidia Deepens Grip on Cloud AI With Major AWS Chip Deal
items.summary: The deal would help scale capacity as AWS builds its own chips, revealing deeper reliance on Nvidia’s stack as usage keeps growing.

items.title: Gemini shares surge 6% after hours on Q4 earnings
items.summary: Gemini reported its fourth quarter revenues were $60.3 million, which co-founders Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss say is its highest quarterly revenue in three years.

items.title: FBI warns fake FBI token on Tron is trying to scam users
items.summary: Scammers impersonating the FBI via a token are telling Tron users they are under investigation and must complete a check to avoid having their assets frozen.

items.title: Solana DApps revenue falls to 18-month low as SOL price risks $80 retest
items.summary: Weakening onchain activity and bearish derivatives data suggest that a SOL price recovery will take longer than most investors anticipate.

items.title: Coinbase, Apex Group tokenize Bitcoin Yield Fund on Base
items.summary: Coinbase Asset Management’s Anthony Bassili says the Bitcoin Yield Fund’s tokenized share class checks “identity and eligibility at the token level” for compliance.

items.title: Bitcoin Trails Money Supply Growth as Energy Costs and Rates Bite
items.summary: Higher fuel costs and restrictive financial conditions are absorbing consumer liquidity, helping explain why expanding global money supply has yet to translate into gains for Bitcoin.

items.title: Execution quality is the missing metric in Bitcoin and Ethereum markets
items.summary: Crypto’s hidden trading costs demand the adoption of transaction cost analysis. Slippage, fees and fragmentation erode trust as crypto matures into institutional markets.

items.title: Bits to Bricks: Can Stablecoins Prevent De-Dollarization?
items.summary: Atlantic Council's Alisha Chhangani joins Bits to Bricks to discuss stablecoins, dollar dominance, CBDCs, and the future of digital currency.

items.title: Bitcoin price tussle at $70K may hint that market bottom is not in
items.summary: Bitcoin slipped below $70K as futures selling rose and spot demand cooled, but a recurring setup on lower time frames points to a potential rebound.

items.title: Crypto Clarity Act inches toward Senate hearing as lawmakers weigh legislative trades
items.summary: The White House may be reviewing fresh legislative text, and lawmakers are reportedly weighing offers to banks of other, unrelated provisions for their support.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Gemini Shares Rise After Hours as Investors Back Shift Beyond Crypto Trading
items.summary: Investors focused on a shift toward steadier revenue streams and a push into prediction markets, even as trading volumes declined.

items.title: SEC interpretation on crypto laws ‘a beginning, not an end,‘ says Atkins
items.summary: The SEC chair signaled that the agency would defer to a market structure bill if passed by Congress, but needed a ”bridge” to clarify crypto regulation.

items.title: BPI sounds alarm on 'backdoor' for hardware wallets in Kentucky crypto bill
items.summary: Provisions in the state legislation violate the core ethos and value proposition of Bitcoin as an asset that can be held in self-custody, the trade group said.

items.title: SOL treasury Forward industries buys back shares using crypto-backed debt
items.summary: The company is leveraging its crypto treasury to fund a share buyback, reducing outstanding shares and potentially boosting per-share value following a six-month slide.

items.title: Microsoft Launches MAI-Image-2 Text-to-Image Model—And It's Better Than Expected
items.summary: Microsoft's AI image generator offers impressive realism and text rendering, but strict content limits and 1:1-only output hold it back.

items.title: EtherFi turns to real-world assets for yield with $25M Plume allocation
items.summary: EtherFi will integrate Plume’s Nest vaults, beginning with exposure to a Superstate-backed fund and expanding to a dedicated RWA vault within its platform.

items.title: Fake FBI Crypto Tokens Are Being Used to Threaten Tron Users, Authorities Warn
items.summary: The FBI warned of a new threat emerging on Tron’s network, with users receiving tokens that aren’t actually from federal law enforcement.

items.title: Solana Treasury Forward Industries Uses Loan to Buy Back Shares After 89% Price Dive
items.summary: Solana treasury firm Forward Industries bought back more than 6 million shares for $27.4 million as it seeks to add shareholder value.

items.title: Telling Your Chatbot You Have a Mental Health Condition Can Change the Answer You Get
items.summary: A study finds that mentioning a mental health condition can increase AI refusals, including on legitimate tasks.

items.title: DeFi risk management giant Gauntlet sees $380 million exit as OKX crypto campaign ends
items.summary: Gauntlet noted that deposits are now back to same levels before the campaign, and has navigated large capital swings before due to incentive campaign endings, airdrops, and shifts in market conditions which regularly produce short-period s…

items.title: Crypto markets – and the American people – deserve clarity
items.summary: The SEC is pivoting away from its previous regulatory strategy.

items.title: Appeals court clears way for Nevada to temporarily ban prediction market Kalshi
items.summary: The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals denied a legal effort by Kalshi to stave off an expected temporary restraining order from the state of Nevada.

items.title: Coinbase's bitcoin yield fund goes onchain with Apex's tokenization push
items.summary: The Coinbase Bitcoin Yield Fund's tokenized share class runs on Base as the $3.5 trillion fund services giant Apex applies tokenization across its business.

items.title: Bybit launches yield-bearing tokenized gold, expanding RWA yield market

items.title: First Working Quantum Battery Proves Bigger Really Does Mean Faster
items.summary: Australian researchers built a battery that charges in femtoseconds, stores energy for nanoseconds, and gets faster the larger it grows—defying every rule conventional batteries follow.

items.summary: The Web3 company will provide capital, advisory support and business development to help Avalanche projects scale, with a focus on real-world assets and digital identity.

items.summary: Casimo will be responsible for growing the Dutch market maker's institutional crypto offering.

items.summary: The platform is designed to enable lending, borrowing and yield on native Bitcoin through onchain financial services.

items.summary: The arrangement between the U.S. derivatives regulator and the professional sports organization is the first of its kind in overseeing sports events contracts.
